_meta: Human-labeled calibration set for the spec-quality judge. ONE JSON object per line; rows with a _meta key are skipped by calibrate.ts. Schema: {specPath, dimension (D1-D6), humanScore (0.0-1.0), note}. 9 specs labeled by hand (R. Rice via Claude, 2026-06-15) spanning a quality spread - strong ends (contract.overview, opportunities.merge API tests, party.record.overview) and weak ends (navigate.spec zero-assertion navs, party.grid row-count-only filters, global.search count-only + fixme) - with both good and weak examples present per dimension. Adjust any score you disagree with; grow the set as the suite evolves.

note: Most tests assert the real claimed outcome (note count +1, email count increases, filter response verified, A-Z sort, deal-summary count >0, ULOB console-error guard). The 'Deleting a call outcome' test only waits on the delete waiter — no post-assert the card is gone — pulling it off a perfect score.
Web-first toPass / toBeVisible / count assertions and apiUtils response verification throughout; the delete test lacks an explicit post-assertion.

CALIBRATION SIGNAL: textbook waiter discipline elsewhere (promise-before-action, before-navigation initial load, before every filter toggle per bug-patterns #18) BUT a hard `await page.waitForTimeout(2000)` at line 18 is a real D3 violation. The judge SHOULD ding this even in the canonical reference — that's why this anchor is mid, not high.
All DOM interaction routed through PartiesOverviewPage / PartyRecordPage methods (good boundary); resilience of the underlying locators can only be fully judged with the POM bodies in the artifact.
All entities via purpose-built dataFactoryParties random samplers (getPartyWithCallOutcome, getPartyWithMultipleEventTypes, etc.); no hardcoded IDs; no destructive shared-data ops.

Business assertions live in the spec, DOM mechanics in the POM; tests are order-independent; no .only. Minor: direct POM locator-property access in the people-filter test (acceptable per /spec-review Check 3).
Mixed: the 3 UI merge tests assert only that the success modal/text appears (do not verify the merge outcome); the 2 duplicate-note API tests + transfer-history test verify real DB state.
Web-first visibility asserts are fine, but the GraphQL tests check res.data.mergeParties.success and never inspect the res.errors / business-error surface.
Preview waiter is created before the click, but there are hard page.waitForTimeout(1000) sleeps (lines 23, 69) and no waiter on the merge-complete operation.
Generic getByRole('combobox') and getByRole('button',{name:'Yes'}) risk collision; partial-string heading match; combobox driven manually with no selectOption.

Destructive keystone violation: the implemented merge tests SAMPLE shared pairs via getPartiesForMerge (ORDER BY NEWID) with no skip-guard, and the fixme hardcodes a single-use pair - not fresh-data-per-run (bug-patterns #25).
Business assertions in the spec, POM is interaction-only, no .only; some order-dependence risk from the shared merge pool.
8 implemented tests each verify a meaningful outcome (Ready/Duplicate/Issue status transitions, save-blocked alert, validation resolution).
Web-first status assertions, but no GraphQL error-surface inspection and the Move-duplicate action has no post-action assertion.
Happy-path upsert waiter is correct, but the duplicate-detection stages and the Move action lack getResponsePromise and rely on timeout:15000 UI polling.

All interactive tests fill Address Line 1 via raw #downshift-0-input with .fill() and NO blur/Tab (the broken Google Places autocomplete pattern); meter/enrollment fills also lack blur. selectOption on State is fine.

Permanent account-move mutations with no skip-guard (pool-depletion warnings in comments) and several hardcoded IDs (party 3744000, account 1234567890); some dynamic timestamp account numbers are good.
No POM abstraction at all - every locator and business assertion is inline raw page.* in the spec body; order-independent and no .only, but the boundary is fully collapsed.
9 implemented tests with specific assertions (confidence/future-rep cleared, flag removed, bucket-rep tasks closed, active-rep tasks NOT closed).
Web-first throughout, but ZERO GraphQL error-surface checks - mutation success is inferred only from the transfer modal closing.

Navigation waiters are solid, but clickTransfer fires the mutation with NO getResponsePromise(OPERATION_TRANSFER_OPPORTUNITY) before it - success is inferred from modal.not.toBeVisible (silent-failure risk).
Extensive getByRole + accessible names; MUI Select internals navigated via xpath (fragile but functional); one generic gridcell.first() on a filtered grid.

Terminal statuses excluded and NEWID sampling (good), but the actor-match gap (bug-patterns #24): the data query returns BucketRepAgentId yet the test transfers to selectFirstAvailableSalesRep without constraining the opp to that current actor - silent no-op risk.
Order-independent, no .only, business assertions in the spec, POMs are primitives; a few scattered raw locators in the spec for context-specific assertions.

18 tests but most filter tests assert only row.count()>0 (filter fired, result never verified against the filter value); status/sort/reset tests do verify content; text-search test asserts only toHaveValue.
Predominantly row-count-or-visibility only; no response-body assertions; the search test has zero grid assertions.

Good promise-before-action in many filter tests, but a hard waitForTimeout(1000) in the segment test, a missing promise before the state fill, and tests 13-18 skip waiters entirely; initial nav not awaited.
Strong getByTestId/getByRole, but a raw MUI css class (.css-1me1b0w, acknowledged TODO), brittle nth() indexing, nth(1) header-row assumption, and state autocomplete not scoped to the listbox.
Hardcoded zip/city/state and party-number codes; zero dataFactory/NEWID; relies entirely on pre-existing DB state.
Order-independent (fresh navigate.toParties per test), no .only, cell-verification logic lives in the GridComponent POM; one raw getByTestId in the spec body.
Test 1 navigates to 4 pages with ZERO assertions ('Add Expect to Page' comments) and asserts only 3 final container-visibility checks; Test 2 asserts URL + heading visibility.
Assertions are container-visibility and URL/heading only - no data or meaningful-state verification.

Inconsistent: toParties/toOpportunitiesGrid use API waiters but toContracts/toSummaryPanel/toPartyRecord/toContractRecord do not; Test 2 has no post-nav waiters and asserts rely on default 5s timeouts.
POMs are testid-first, but the spec body embeds 6 raw page.locator('h5').filter() queries and the POMs carry fragile fallbacks (.MuiCard-root, xpath, nth-child).
Hardcoded entity IDs (party 32543188, contract 69735); no dataFactory/NEWID sampling - brittle against data resets.
Order-independent and no .only, but 6 raw locators in the spec body violate the POM boundary and Test 1 does pure navigation with no assertions.
3 implemented tests with concrete outcomes - UI merge verified by snackbar + a row disappearing + a DB MergedOpportunities record; cross-bucket negative and same-bucket positive checked via payload.
T1 is web + DB; T2/T3 check payload shape (validationMessages, survivor/victim) but never inspect res.data.mergeOpportunities.errors.
Waiter order is correct (promise before nav, then grid-refresh waiter); no waitForTimeout anti-patterns; API tests post directly which is fine.
Buttons/snackbar use getByTestId/getByRole (good), but the opportunity row + checkbox use raw [data-id=...] CSS attribute selectors (brittle if the grid restructures).

Split: T2/T3 are exemplary fresh-data-per-run (createParty + insertOpportunity, disposable) - the model for destructive specs; T1 hardcodes shared opp IDs with only a passive test.skip guard, dragging the score.
Clean POM/spec boundary, business assertions in the spec, no cross-page nav from the POM, no .only.
Vacuous: asserts getResultCount()>0 only, never that the searched entity appears in the results; the party-name branch silently passes when the name is null.
Three count-based assertions only, no web-first DOM checks; the POM's verifyResultContainsText() exists but is never called.
Waiter setup is correct (promise before fill) but it uses bare search() instead of searchUntilFound() despite the 2-4s index lag - the test is in fact fixme'd because of a search-waiter timeout.
getByTestId for the input + standard MUI list-item class; correct MUI idioms; POM boundary maintained.
sqlDB.getRandomParty/getRandomContract random sampling, fresh per run, no hardcoded IDs.
Order-independent with clearSearch resets, no .only, business assertions in the spec, no raw locators in the spec body.
Both tests are semantic and non-vacuous - Finance ID numeric match against the Industrial DB, and edit+save persistence verified by reopening the flyout.
Web-first state-change assertions and an HTTP 200+ surface check on the save (REST endpoint, so GraphQL error-surface is n/a).
Strong mutation sync (getResponsePromise before click, correct operation); weaker on initial load - navigate.toContractRecord lacks a getResponsePromise, mitigated by verifyContractFullscreenLoaded.
All locators are data-testid; minor nth=0 + .first() chaining workaround; RHF friendly-name fill has no blur/Tab (potential validation-trigger gap).
Timestamp value for the mutation; hardcoded contract/party IDs are documented stable CI fixtures (GOT GRACE LLC); T1 has no party-scoped CI filter but self-recovers by walking candidates.
Order-independent and idempotent (restores the original value), no .only, business assertions in the spec, clean POM boundary.
